write a python function to determine if a given musical note sequence can be sung within a specific vocal range the input will be a list of strings where each string represents a musical note e.g c4 g5 etc and two strings representing the lower and upper bounds of the singer's vocal range e g a3 and f5 the function should return a boolean value true if all notes in the sequence fall within the specified vocal range and false otherwise

the function should check if each note in the list is within the specified range by converting the note to a numerical value and comparing it to the range values

the function should first convert each note to its corresponding numerical value based on the note name and octave

then compare each numerical value to the lower and upper bounds to determine if it falls within the range

if all notes are within the range, return true, otherwise return false

the function should be implemented in python and should handle any valid note format

the function should be tested with the provided examples to ensure correctness

the function should be efficient and handle large input sizes without performance issues

the function should be well-documented to explain its purpose and how it works

the function should be tested with additional cases to ensure it handles all possible scenarios

the function should be used in a real-world scenario to check if a piece of music is suitable for practice based on vocal range

the function should be integrated into a larger system to manage vocal health and music practice

the function should be maintained and updated as new note formats are added

the function should be used in a choir setting to ensure that members can sing within their vocal range

the function should be used to help choirs plan their practice sessions based on vocal capabilities

the function should be used to ensure that music is suitable for all members of the choir

the function should be used to help choirs select pieces that are within the vocal range of all members

the function should be used to help choirs determine which pieces are suitable for practice
